title: 'Organisational arrogance, grassroots disconnect': Rumblings of revolt in TMC after poll debacle
content: Trinamool Congress faces internal turmoil as former MLA Raj Chakraborty quits politics, citing the end of his tenure. This follows public criticism from ex-ministers and functionaries blaming leadership arrogance and factionalism for the poll defeat. Several leaders have voiced discontent over party decisions and alleged pressure from within.

title: 'Let us know': VCK attacks Vijay's 'arrogance' over WhatsApp support messages
content: Viduthalai Chiruthaigal Katchi (VCK) criticizes actor-politician Vijay's TVK for seeking government support via WhatsApp messages instead of direct consultations. VCK questions Vijay's political handling and accuses his party of misguiding him and exploiting his popularity while also criticizing TVK's stance on dynastic politics and resort politics.

title: Dig at Cong? Akhilesh shares Mamata, Stalin photos with 'not ones who abandon' post
content: Akhilesh's remark was in the backdrop of the Congress snapping ties with ally DMK in Tamil Nadu and extending support to actor Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), which has added to rancour in the INDIA bloc. The 2026 assembly elections in Tamil Nadu and West Bengal have cast a foreboding shadow on the NDA's rival bloc as  Mamata and Stalin were two powerful regional leaders in the anti-BJP camp.

title: Dhoni tops taxpayer list as Jharkhand-Bihar see Rs 20,000 crore tax collection
content: Former India captain MS Dhoni has been revealed as the highest individual taxpayer from Jharkhand and Bihar combined for the 2025-26 financial year. The Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax confirmed Dhoni's top position, though the exact tax amount remains undisclosed. This achievement highlights Dhoni's significant financial contribution to the region.

title: Crude timing: $7 billion 'unusual' oil bets placed ahead of Iran war announcements
content: Massive oil market bets, totaling up to $7 billion, were placed just before significant US President Trump announcements concerning Iran.  Traders executed large sell orders minutes before key political developments, leading to substantial price drops.  This unusual timing and scale have triggered regulatory scrutiny, with investigators examining potential insider trading concerns.

title: Google CEO Sundar Pichai on when he found 'the calmest place in the world'
content: Sundar Pichai, CEO of Google and Alphabet, is known for his calm leadership, a style that proved crucial during the AI race. Despite criticism following ChatGPT's viral success, Pichai maintained a steady approach, focusing on internal quality. This strategic patience, cultivated over a decade, has positioned Google's diverse projects for a future centered on AI.

title: Nvidia share falls to zero in China; CEO says latest chips shouldn’t reach Beijing
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang declared America a priority for advanced chip sales, stating China should not have access to the "latest and greatest" AI technology. However, he urged the US not to abandon the Chinese market entirely, arguing that global sales and increased tax revenues strengthen American economic and national security.

title: Pakistan takes a risky gamble, skips purchasing spot LNG, bets Hormuz tensions will ease
content: Pakistan is foregoing urgent LNG purchases, betting on a swift de-escalation of Middle East tensions and the timely arrival of cheaper Qatari shipments. This risky strategy comes as the country faces a significant gas deficit and widespread electricity outages, exacerbated by disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Explainer: Can hantavirus outbreak become Covid 2.0?
content: Health authorities are urgently tracing passengers from the MV Hondius cruise ship following a hantavirus outbreak that has claimed three lives. The Andes strain, capable of human-to-human transmission, was identified, prompting isolation and contact tracing. While not comparable to COVID-19, the virus requires close contact to spread.

title: 'You have 10 working days to voluntarily recognise unions': Google UK staff tells company
content: Google's DeepMind employees are demanding union recognition amid concerns over their AI technology's use by the US and Israeli militaries. Workers seek a commitment against developing AI weapons and surveillance tools, urging management to voluntarily recognize their unions or face legal action. The move highlights growing anxieties about AI's ethical implications and its potential for harm.

title: 'Raja Shivaji' BO collection day 8: Riteish Deshmukh film sets sight on Rs 75 crore on second weekend
content: 'Raja Shivaji', directed by and starring Riteish Deshmukh as Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, has delivered the greatest opening week in Marathi cinema history with Rs. 52.65 crore India net in Week 1 and is chasing the Rs. 75 crore India net target as its second weekend begins. Released on Maharashtra Day (May 1, 2026), the Rs. 100 crore historical epic — the most expensive Marathi film ever made features Sanjay Dutt, Abhishek Bachchan, Vidya Balan, Genelia Deshmukh and the debut of Rahyl Deshmukh, with Salman Khan's still-viral surprise cameo as Jiva Mahala continuing to drive footfalls.

title: BCCI warns IPL teams over honey-trap risks, tightens hotel room access rules
content: The BCCI has issued a stern warning to IPL franchises regarding 'targeted compromise and honey-trapping,' enhancing security and access protocols. Stricter rules now govern hotel room access, unauthorised departures, and accreditation display. Franchise owners face new restrictions on interacting with players during matches, and vaping is strictly prohibited, with severe penalties for violations.

title: Retired IPS officer’s wife found murdered inside Jubilee Hills home, domestic help under lens
content: Tragedy struck in Hyderabad's Jubilee Hills as the wife of former IPS officer Vinay Ranjan Ray was found murdered. Authorities suspect a recently hired domestic worker from Nepal and her accomplices, who allegedly stole valuables and fled. The victim was found on the first floor of the family's residence. Police are actively pursuing the absconding suspects.

title: Delhi Capitals still haunted by 'mental scar' of GT loss: Ex-India cricketer
content: Former India spinner Murali Kartik feels Delhi Capitals' IPL campaign was derailed by a one-run loss to Gujarat Titans, leaving them mentally scarred. Despite a decent squad, DC has struggled since that defeat, experiencing extreme results. Now seventh, they face a resurgent Kolkata Knight Riders, who are in winning form.

title: India is making its 'dumb bombs' smart: What DRDO’s TARA glide kit means for the IAF
content: India's indigenous glide weapon system, TARA, achieved a successful maiden flight, transforming unguided bombs into precision-guided munitions. Developed by DRDO and tested from an IAF Jaguar, TARA offers enhanced accuracy and stand-off capability at low cost. This development aligns with global trends, boosting India's affordable precision strike power and airpower.

title: Vijay under pressure, DMK-AIADMK scramble to stop TVK from forming govt: TN chaos in 10 points
content: Tamil Nadu's political landscape shifted dramatically as Vijay's TVK emerged as the largest party, winning 108 seats but falling short of a majority. Intense negotiations followed, with Congress backing TVK, causing friction with the DMK. Accusations of gubernatorial pressure surfaced, while both TVK and AIADMK reportedly engaged in resort politics amid a complex government formation battle.

title: US strikes down Trump's 10% tariffs — How should India proceed now?
content: US courts have again blocked Trump's 10% global tariffs, deeming them unauthorized. This ruling, following earlier tariff invalidations, signals a return to WTO's MFN rates. For India, this policy instability raises concerns, prompting advice to pause Free Trade Agreement talks until US trade systems stabilize, preventing one-sided deals.

title: BTech student chased, stabbed multiple times over relationship in Hyderabad, dies
content: A 23-year-old BTech student was brutally stabbed to death in Secunderabad's Chilakalguda, allegedly due to a long-standing love affair. The victim was attacked by six unidentified men on motorcycles. Protests erupted as the victim's family accused the woman's family of orchestrating the murder.

title: Rs 27 crore, one big question - What has happened to Rishabh Pant in T20s?
content: Rishabh Pant’s unbeaten 32 helped Lucknow Super Giants beat Royal Challengers Bengaluru by nine runs in Lucknow, but questions over his T20 form continue to grow. Once seen as one of India’s brightest white-ball stars, Pant has struggled for consistency in recent IPL seasons despite becoming the league’s most expensive player at Rs 27 crore. His falling returns in T20 cricket, changing role at LSG and growing competition for India spots have added to the pressure, with Irfan Pathan also admitting the wicketkeeper-batter is under scrutiny.

title: Virat shares heartwarming moment with Prince after pacer removes him for duck
content: Virat Kohli shared a warm moment with young LSG pacer Prince Yadav after being dismissed by a brilliant nip-backer. Yadav revealed Kohli himself had advised him to stick to that length after a previous match. Despite the dismissal, Kohli appeared relaxed and appreciative of the youngster's performance.

title: Horrific murder on CCTV: Woman chased, stabbed to death by husband in Telangana
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Suryapet district as a 24-year-old housewife was brutally stabbed to death by her estranged husband at a tea stall. The victim, Sirisha, had left her abusive husband, Manideep, over marital disputes. Despite police intervention for counselling, Manideep ambushed and fatally attacked her in public, leading to her death.

title: Who will govern Tamil Nadu? TVK MLAs threaten mass resignation; AIADMK MLAs holed up in resort
content: Tamil Nadu's political landscape is in flux as Vijay's TVK emerged as the largest party but fell short of a majority. Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar has sought proof of support, sparking criticism from TVK allies who demand a floor test. Meanwhile, DMK and AIADMK are reportedly exploring an alliance to counter TVK's surge.

title: Harassed in relationship, Class 12 student dies by suicide in Gurgaon
content: A 17-year-old student tragically ended her life in Gurgaon, reportedly due to harassment in a suspected relationship. Her suicide note details feelings of being misunderstood and disrespected by a youth she knew for a year, along with an alleged incident at tuition. Police are investigating, having contacted the boy mentioned and retrieved chats.

title: Eye on future: Shreyas Iyer likely to replace Suryakumar Yadav as India T20I captain
content: Indian selectors are set to remove Suryakumar Yadav as India's T20I captain when the squads for the Ireland and England series are picked. Due to his batting struggles, the selectors have run out of patience with the 35-year-old. Shreyas Iyer is a strong contender to take over the leadership role as the team looks for a fresh start ahead of a packed T20I calendar.

title: ISI-linked module busted: Delhi temple, military camp, UP police stations were on target list
content: Pakistan's ISI allegedly plotted terror attacks in Delhi and Haryana, targeting a historic temple, a popular highway dhaba, and a military camp. Operatives linked to the Shahzad Bhatti module conducted reconnaissance, sharing photos and videos with handlers. The plan aimed to cause panic, disruption, and mass casualties, with police stations in Uttar Pradesh also on the radar.

title: Railway cop tied to pole, lynched by mob in police presence for rape bid
content: A railway constable was lynched by a mob on Thursday in Bhubaneswar after allegedly attempting to rape a woman. The incident, captured on video, saw the deceased tied to a pole before being declared dead at the hospital. Police have arrested four individuals and are identifying others involved.

title: One year after Operation Sindoor, how India is still keeping Pakistan under pressure
content: India's Operation Sindoor, though its kinetic phase has ended, continues to exert pressure on Pakistan. Following successful strikes on terror infrastructure and air defenses, India remains vigilant, ready to retaliate against any future provocations. Beyond military action, diplomatic and economic measures are actively impacting Islamabad, ensuring Pakistan remains militarily unbalanced and countering its narrative.

title: Oil at $101 but could Strait of Hormuz crisis push prices to $200?
content: Escalating Middle East conflict has choked the world's busiest oil passage, pushing crude prices past $100 per barrel with warnings of a surge to $167 or higher. A prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz threatens a severe energy and trade shock, potentially reducing global trade growth significantly and deepening economic uncertainty worldwide.

title: Vinesh Phogat cleared WADA’s retirement rule, but Asiad dream looks out of bounds now
content: Wrestler Vinesh Phogat has reportedly met WADA's six-month notice requirement for returning athletes, according to sources. Despite this, her comeback is facing scrutiny over registration issues and a whereabouts failure. Her eligibility for upcoming events, including the Asian Games, remains uncertain due to these controversies and selection policies.

title: 'Vomited major toxin’: How rat poison inside watermelon led to death of 4 in Mumbai
content: Rat poison, specifically zinc phosphide, has been identified as the cause of death for four members of the Dokadia family in Mumbai. Forensic tests confirmed the poison was inside the watermelon they consumed. Police are now investigating whether the incident was accidental, suicidal, or homicidal, examining all possibilities.

title: Rupee in red: Currency falls 45 paise to 94.67 against US dollar
content: The Indian rupee opened sharply lower against the US dollar, falling 36 paise to 94.58 due to rising Middle East tensions. This marked a reversal from Thursday's gains, as US President Trump warned Iran of stronger military action.

title: Watch: Muslim jail officer marries ex-murder convict; 'kanyadaan' performed by Bajrang Dal leader
content: An assistant jail superintendent and a former life convict, who met while he was serving his sentence at Satna Central Jail, have married. Their love story, which began with official interactions, culminated in a Hindu wedding ceremony in Chhatarpur district, becoming a local sensation.

title: ‘Line ke bahar daal …’: Pant’s on-field masterplan removes Tim David — Watch
content: Rishabh Pant's sharp tactical advice proved pivotal in Lucknow Super Giants' narrow nine-run DLS victory over Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Caught on camera instructing spinner Shahbaz Ahmed, Pant's guidance led to the crucial dismissal of the dangerous Tim David. This breakthrough shifted momentum, ultimately helping LSG end their losing streak and revive playoff aspirations.

title: First time in 1110 days! Virat Kohli falls for duck as Prince Yadav stuns RCB
content: Prince Yadav's stunning 140.4 kph nip-backer, inspired by Virat Kohli's own advice, shattered the RCB star's stumps, marking Kohli's first IPL duck in over three years. This pivotal moment, coupled with Mitchell Marsh's century, propelled Lucknow Super Giants to a nine-run DLS victory over Royal Challengers Bengaluru, keeping LSG's playoff dreams alive.

title: AIADMK-DMK Tie-Up Buzz Amid Vijay Suspense: What's Next For Tamil Nadu?
content: Vijay's TVK won 108 seats in the April election but fell 10 short of majority, the scenario leading to chaos in Tamil Nadu as the actor tries to rally the extra numbers while his rivals - the DMK and AIADMK - plot a historic alliance to keep him out

title: 800-Year-Old Shiva Temple Demolished For School In Telangana, Case Filed
content: An 800-year-old Kakatiya Shiva temple in Telangana was demolished, prompting outrage and legal action.

title: One Year Of Operation Sindoor: How India's Defence Tech Is Rewriting War
content: Operation Sindoor 1st Anniversary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh recently described Operation Sindoor as proof of India's tech-driven military might.

title: Over 1 Lakh Schools Lack Power, 98,500 Without Girls' Toilet: Niti Aayog Report
content: Niti Aayog Report: As many as 98,592 schools in the country do not have functional girls' toilets while 61,540 don't have any usable toilet.

title: West Bengal Government Formation LIVE: Amit Shah Arrives In Kolkata As BJP Prepares For Swearing-In Tomorrow
content: West Bengal Government Formation LIVE Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Union Home Minister Amit Shah, BJP president Nitin Nabin, along with several Union ministers, are expected to attend the swearing-in ceremony.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE Updates: UAE Reports New Strikes As US-Iran Ceasefire Hangs By Thread
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: A US admiral said US forces sank six small Iranian ships. Iran denied any had been sunk and earlier fired warning shots at US warships

title: Ex-China Defence Ministers Get Suspended Death Sentences In Corruption Cases
content: Chinese President Xi Jinping's anti-corruption drive has resulted in the removal of China's highest ranking general and military leadership.

title: How Saudi's Defiance Pushed Trump To Halt 'Project Freedom' In Hormuz
content: Saudi Arabia is frustrated with the American commander-in-chief's "erratic handling of the war", due to which Middle Eastern nations, especially the United Arab Emirates (UAE), have borne the brunt of Iran's retaliatory strikes.

title: Telangana Engineering Student Stabbed 15 Times, Allegedly By Lover's Brother
content: According to locals, nearly 10 people arrived on motorcycles, confronted Yuvan and entered into an argument before stabbing him 15 times with knives

title: Tamil Nadu Government Formation LIVE:  Cannot Tie-Up With Any “Communal Party": DMK Asks AIADMK To Dump BJP
content: Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026 Live Updates: There is buzz that the Dravidian arch-rivals DMK and AIADMK may come together after over 50 years to form a government and keep Vijay out of power.

title: Life Insurance vs Fixed Deposit: The Winner On Returns May Surprise You
content: Over long horizons, equity exposure and compounding can deliver returns that beat traditional fixed deposits -- especially after tax.

title: Royal Family Seeks Action On Sabarimala Issues From New Kerala Government
content: PN Narayana Varma, representative of the Pandalam royal family, demanded that cases registered against Sabarimala devotees during the protests be withdrawn without delay.

title: "Gundagardi": Mother Of Suvendu Adhikari's Aide Blames Trinamool For Murder
content: Calling for justice, Chandranath Rath's mother demanded life imprisonment for the gunmen over death sentence.

title: Mamata Banerjee Stays Defiant, Assembly Dissolved: What Are Her Options Now?
content: As Mamata Banerjee continues to claim wrong doing in Assembly Elections, it has been anticipated the legal battle over Bengal might escalate to courts.

title: All 107 TVK MLAs To Resign If DMK, AIADMK Try To Form Government: Sources
content: The TVK, which won 108 seats -- two of them won by Vijay -- has 107 MLAs and argues that as the single-largest party, it should be invited by the Governor to form government.

title: Is the war over or not? US-Iran trade fire amid ceasefire, UAE hit again; where things stand
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: Scientists reveal how black holes grow into cosmic monsters via mergers
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. Join us as we craft knowledge with precision and passion, bringing you on a journey where the mysteries of the universe unfold with every word. Read More

title: ‘Who will trust us again?’ Mani Shankar Aiyar tears into Congress-TVK alliance gamble
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. title: Tamil Nadu government formation LIVE: Left parties hold executive committee meetings; DMK seeks change in Lok Sabha seating arrangement

title: TVK chief Vijay should be given opportunity to prove majority in Tamil Nadu Assembly: Jammu & Kashmir CM Omar Abdullah
content: “So, if we take the Supreme Court judgment, then the Tamil Nadu Lok Bhavan has no justification to stop this process” Jammu & Kashmir CM Omar Abdullah said

title: ECI publishes Assembly poll index cards, statistical reports in record 72 hrs
content: The data release covers all 830 Assembly constituencies where the election process concluded on May 6

title: India at Venice Biennale 2026 curtain raiser: Many voices, one resonance
content: From soil and thread to immersive myth and monumental sculpture, India’s Venice Biennale 2026 presence expands beyond the pavilion into a layered meditation on home and identity

title: Maharashtra Class 10 results out; over 92% students pass, girls outperform boys again
content: Girls secured an overall pass percentage of 94.96 as against boys' 89.56%, board chairman Trigun Kulkarni said

title: Smacks of low political opportunism: Mani Shankar Aiyar slams Congress’ TVK tie-up decision
content: Mani Shankar Aiyar said he cannot imagine the fathers of the Indian National Congress blessing such "politics of expediency"

title: GBA elections in Bengaluru to be held between June 14 and 24: State Election Commissioner
content: Inputs were also taken from the commissioners of the five corporations, who serve as ex-officio members, before moving ahead with the scheduling exercise.

title: Kerala polls: LDF will overcome setback and make strong comeback, says CPI(M) leader E.P. Jayarajan
content: In a Facebook post, Jayarajan says the scale of the defeat suffered by Left Democratic Front in Assembly election was “completely unexpected”

title: Can't be called backstabbing, what's the issue if like-minded parties forming govt in Tamil Nadu: Congress
content: The Congress, a long-time ally of the DMK, announced its support to actor-politician Vijay's TVK to form the government in Tamil Nadu and severed ties with the Dravidian major

title: BJP won’t support any coalition formation in Tamil Nadu: Nainar Nagenthran
content: The BJP, as part of the AIADMK-led National Democratic Alliance, contested 27 seats. The party’s nominee M. Bhojarajan won by a slim margin of 976 votes

title: Chandrababu Naidu directs officials to clear e-files within 24 hours, push digital governance
content: The Chief Minister says the speed of governance depends on the speed of file disposal, and asks departments to move almost entirely to e-office systems

title: Akhilesh reaffirms support to TMC, DMK; takes dig at Congress
content: Akhilesh Yadav alleges that the Bengal polls witnessed "multi-layered mafia tactics" involving the BJP, the Election Commission and "underground elements.

title: CUET-UG advisory on dress code, permissible items released
content: The advisory further stated that white light clothing is preferred and candidates may wear woollens if required, subject to reporting early for frisking

title: India faces no immediate public health threat from hantavirus: NIV chief
content: Hantaviruses are mainly transmitted to humans through contact with infected rodents or their excreta such as saliva, urine and faeces, he told PTI

title: Left’s declining electoral strength a major concern, hopeful of revival, says D. Raja
content: The CPI general secretary said his party would soon decide its stand on whom to support in Tamil Nadu, and warned that the BJP forming a government in West Bengal posed challenges not just for the state but for the entire country.

title: West Bengal government formation LIVE: Amit Shah arrives in Kolkata for BJP legislature party meet to elect new CM
content: The swearing-in ceremony of the State's first BJP government is scheduled to be held at Brigade Parade Ground on May 9

title: Former IPS officer’s wife found dead at Jubilee Hills residence in Hyderabad, Nepal gang role suspected
content: Preliminary findings suggest that the gang gained entry into the house with the alleged assistance of a woman domestic help

title: Tamil Nadu government formation: Lone TVK cadre stages protest outside Lok Bhavan
content: Police identified the man as Suresh, an assistant of film director S.A. Chandrasekar, father of Mr. Vijay.

title: Writer T. Padmanabhan backs K.C. Venugopal for Kerala Chief Minister’s post
content: Padmanabhan says he has “close relations with many Congress leaders, but is personally closest” to Venugopal

title: Delhi tops crimes against foreigners, among Union Territories in 2024, confirms National Crime Records Bureau
content: Together, the Union Territories registered 67 such cases in the year, with Delhi alone contributing 64 cases, Jammu and Kashmir reported two cases, while the Andaman and Nicobar Islands recorded one case

title: Watch: Vijay won, but why can’t he become Chief Minister yet?
content: For the first time since 1952, Tamil Nadu has delivered a fractured mandate in an Assembly election. The man behind this turn of events is cinema superstar Vijay, who not only entered politics but also stunned the two Dravidian rivals, the DMK and AIADMK. But Vijay's TVK managed only 108 seats on its own, short of the majority mark of 118. Vijay will need the support of smaller parties to form the government and with the Congress in the mix, it poses a very interesting situation. So, what are the scenarios? What role does the Governor play?

title: West Bengal tops States in crimes by foreigners; Bangladeshis top accused: NCRB
content: The National Crime Records Bureau data showed that crimes against foreigners rose from 238 cases in 2023 to 257 in 2024

title: 'Bar has much to offer': CJI Kant shares anecdote as he dismisses lawyer's plea for revaluation
content: The CJI recounted his time as a final-year law student when he, too, wanted to become a judicial officer, had cleared the written test, and was to appear for the interview

title: In just four months of 2026, Tamil Nadu records 2.63 lakh dog bites, 17 deaths
content: A total of 62,000 dog bite cases were recorded each in January and February, and it rose to 71,000 in March and dipping slightly to 68,000 in April; half of last year’s deaths has been recorded in just four months this year

title: Srikakulam pushes Badi Pilustondi drive to lift admissions in government schools
content: The district administration is worried about a possible drop in admissions in 2026-27 as corporate schools expand into semi-urban areas with bus facilities

title: Telangana’s 2BHK dreams caught between delays, distance and vacancies
content: Several beneficiaries of Telangana’s double-bedroom housing scheme continue to wait for their allotted homes as construction remains incomplete. For several low-income families, rising rents and cost of living have deepened financial stress, turning the dream of a permanent house into an urgent necessity. But in a contrasting trend, several allotted flats in Hyderabad remain unoccupied. What steps is the government taking to complete pending works and improve occupancy in the housing colonies? K. Shiva Shanker, P. Laxma Reddy and P. Sridhar uncover the ground realities

title: CPI, CPI(M) meetings begin to decide on extending support for government formation in Tamil Nadu
content: The Viduthalai Chiruthaigal Katchi’s decision on the issue would depend on the outcome of the meetings of the Left parties

title: Tamil Nadu government formation: After Semmalai, AIADMK MP Thambi Durai too says Palaniswami will come back to power
content: The AIADMK veteran said during the election tour of the general secretary, the public sentiment was evident in favour of his party’s return to power.

title: Dead foetus kept in womb ‘for 22 hours’ in Maharashtra’s Beed; health dept. forms committee to probe
content: Family of the woman has alleged that the doctor attempted to hide the negligence and gain financial benefits

title: Amit Shah arrives in Kolkata to attend BJP's legislature party meeting, swearing in of new govt
content: Mr. Shah was received at the Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose International Airport by BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari

title: India pushes for inclusive migration, stronger anti-trafficking laws at United Nations General Assembly
content: Kirti Singh highlighted the various extensive initiatives undertaken by India, including the use of digital technologies, to support its nationals abroad

title: Bottom 50% of Nagaland households receive only 18% of total income, says report
content: Nagaland recorded a Gini coefficient of 0.46, a measure commonly used for income inequality, where 0 represents perfect equality and 1 indicates extreme inequality

title: Delhi HC to pass interim order protecting Shashi Tharoor’s personality rights over deepfake videos
content: Justice Mini Pushkarna also issued notices to defendants in suit including social media platforms such as X and Meta, owner of Instagram

title: Scope of legal fiction in party mergers
content: Henry Maine called legal fiction an agency by which law adapts to changing societies. In the Indian context, a 1955 ruling delineating the scope of legal fiction, reaffirmed by the Supreme Court in March, sets limits that the law on defections has yet to absorb.

title: Kerala polls: Congress has democratic process for CM selection unlike BJP and CPI(M), says Sandeep Varier
content: Sandeep Varier, who was a BJP leader before joining Congress in 2024, contested on a Congress ticket in the recently concluded Assembly election in Kerala

title: New invasive moth threatens Ladakh’s farm-based economy
content: The larvae of the Phalera cf. bucephala, commonly called the buff-tip moth, are voracious feeders, capable of stripping an entire tree bare in a few weeks

title: Lakhimpur Kheri case: Supreme Court expresses disappointment over non-production of witnesses
content: It asked the trial judge to make an endeavour to conclude the trial in a time-bound manner, and also to file a status report before it

title: Woman killed, several injured in multi-vehicle collision at Kowdiar in Kerala capital
content: A speeding car lost control, veered onto pavement and crashed into three other cars and two two-wheelers, according to eyewitnesses

title: DMK explores backing AIADMK, Tamil Nadu Governor tells Vijay he hasn’t established majority
content: The CPI(M), CPI and the VCK hold the key to any further churn in Tamil Nadu politics; in fact, their combined support is crucial for both the TVK and the AIADMK to form a government

title: Father alleges foul play in daughter’s death at Krosuru police station in Palnadu district
content: The woman had filed a complaint in 2021 against a police constable. The Palnadu SP says she fell from the first floor of the building during the inquiry

title: Class 12 State Board exams: Tamil Nadu registers 95.2% pass rate
content: Erode district recorded the highest pass percentage of 98.8, while Sivaganga district stood second with 98.05% pass, as per the data

title: Caught between forests and fear: Karnataka’s growing human–animal conflict crisis
content: From Kodagu’s hills to Hassan’s farmlands, rising encounters with elephants, gaurs and big cats are claiming lives, exposing gaps in mitigation, and leaving residents demanding urgent, lasting solutions, find Darshan Devaiah B.P. and G.T. Sathish

title: India carries out maiden flight-trial of new weapon system TARA
content: The Tactical Advanced Range Augmentation system was tested on May 7, according to the Defence Ministry

title: Palaniswami saddened over Stalin’s loss from Kolathur constituency, says Puducherry leader Anbalagan
content: Confirming that some newly elected legislators of the AIADMK from Tamil Nadu are accommodated in a resort in Puducherry on the ‘directions’ of Mr. Palaniswami, Mr. Anbalgan said that all legislators are united under the leadership of party general secretary

title: Supreme Court seeks Centre’s response on AAP plea about blocking of its Meta accounts
content: The AAP said it was not informed of any specific content alleged to be objectionable or the statutory provision which was relied upon for blocking the accounts

title: Full list of Bihar cabinet and council of ministers
content: Altogether, 32 Ministers drawn from all constituents of the five-party NDA in the State were administered the oath of office by Governor Lt. Gen. (Retd.) Syed Ata Hasnain
